title: Torenia alata
taxonomy: Torenia alata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Linderniaceae, commonly known as the torenia or wishbone flower. This plant is native to Asia and belongs to the order Lamiales. Within the Linderniaceae family, Torenia alata is placed in the genus Torenia, which includes several other species of flowering plants. The Linderniaceae family is a small group of plants that are closely related to the family Scrophulariaceae. The classification of Torenia alata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Lamiales order.

intro: Torenia alata, also known as the blue wing torenia or blue throatwort, is a flowering plant species in the family Linderniaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Torenia alata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in some herbal formulations, such as cold and flu remedies, and digestive tonics.
